stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key AI/ML topics including transformers, llama, and text-generation.
This indicates ongoing community interest and performance tracking for a major LLM model.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under text-generation pipelines, indicating its primary function as a language model.
The associated tags confirm its relevance within the broader transformer and LLM ecosystem.

stories.title: Mira Murati tells the court that she couldn’t trust Sam Altman’s words
stories.author: Jay Peters
bullet_points: OpenAI's former CTO, Mira Murati, testified under oath regarding Sam Altman.
Murati alleged that Altman lied to her concerning the safety standards of a new AI model.
The testimony was presented during the ongoing Musk v. Altman trial, highlighting internal governance issues at OpenAI.

stories.title: TSMC taps wind power as AI chip demand soars, Taiwan feels energy crunch
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: TSMC is integrating wind power into its operations to meet soaring energy demands.
The surge in energy consumption is directly linked to the massive global demand for AI chips.
The situation highlights a critical energy infrastructure challenge facing Taiwan's semiconductor industry.

stories.title: Design Conductor 2.0: An agent builds a TurboQuant inference accelerator in 80 hours
stories.author: David Chin
bullet_points: Design Conductor 2.0 is an advanced multi-agent system capable of autonomously building complex hardware accelerators.
The system successfully built a TurboQuant inference accelerator in just 80 hours, demonstrating significant capability scaling from prior work.
The advancement is attributed to the integration of frontier LLMs (released April 2026) into the agent harness, enabling handling of much larger and higher-quality tasks.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the 'deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1' model on Hugging Face.
The model is categorized under text-generation and conversational AI pipelines.
It utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors, indicating a focus on practical model deployment.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, has integrated MLX for performance optimization.
This update specifically targets Apple Silicon, leveraging Apple's native ML framework.
The feature is currently available in a preview state, indicating ongoing development.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed for local execution.
It integrates messaging apps with local AI coding agents.
The core feature emphasizes running AI agents entirely on the user's own device for privacy/control.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for running coding tools locally or in the cloud.
It supports various coding models such as Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is collaborating with OpenAI and ROOST to integrate gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models.
These models are designed for safety classification tasks and are available in 20B and 120B sizes.
The models are released under the permissive Apache 2.0 license, promoting open-source safety tooling.

stories.title: Low-Cost Black-Box Detection of LLM Hallucinations via Dynamical System Prediction
stories.author: Mohamed Akrout
bullet_points: Proposes a novel, low-cost method to detect LLM hallucinations by treating the LLM as a black-box dynamical system.
Avoids computationally expensive traditional methods like sampling-based consistency checks or external knowledge retrieval.
The technique involves projecting LLM responses into a high-dimensional manifold to characterize response sequences.

stories.title: The Impossibility Triangle of Long-Context Modeling
stories.author: Yan Zhou
bullet_points: Identifies a fundamental, provable trade-off in long-context modeling for sequence models.
The 'Impossibility Triangle' dictates that models cannot simultaneously achieve high Efficiency, Compactness, and Recall.
Formalizes this trade-off across various architectures, including Transformers and State Space Models.

stories.title: AI Agent Security Has a Runtime Blind Spot, and Most Scanners Still Miss It
stories.author: maxmendes91
bullet_points: Identifies a critical security vulnerability in AI agents related to runtime execution.
Highlights that current security scanning tools are insufficient for detecting this specific blind spot.
Indicates an urgent need for advanced security solutions tailored to dynamic AI agent behavior.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The content highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under standard NLP/ML pipelines like 'text-generation'.
Associated technologies include transformers, PyTorch, and safetensors, indicating a focus on large language models (LLMs).

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports compatibility with the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage models like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
The feature bridges the gap between open-source local models and proprietary API capabilities.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex, when integrated, can read, modify, and execute code within a user's directory.
The integration supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: SpaceX is starting to move on from the world's most successful rocket
stories.author: Stephen Clark
bullet_points: SpaceX is beginning a transition away from its most established launch operations.
Vandenberg Space Force Base in California is slated to become a primary, high-volume launch site for SpaceX.
The article signals a strategic shift in SpaceX's launch infrastructure utilization.

stories.title: Anthropic raises Claude Code usage limits, credits new deal with SpaceX
stories.author: Samuel Axon
bullet_points: Anthropic is increasing usage limits for its Claude Code model.
The company has secured a new partnership with SpaceX.
This deal aligns with Anthropic's existing partnerships with major tech players like Microsoft and Amazon.

stories.title: Report: SpaceX IPO gives Musk unchecked power and forbids investor lawsuits
stories.author: Jon Brodkin
bullet_points: SpaceX's potential IPO structure reportedly includes a waiver for investors.
This waiver would prevent investors from suing the company.
The report suggests this arrangement could grant Elon Musk unchecked power over the firm.

stories.title: Google DeepMind partners with EVE Online for AI model testing
stories.author: Kyle Orland
bullet_points: Google DeepMind is collaborating with EVE Online for testing purposes of its AI models.
The partnership occurs as CCP Games undergoes a major corporate transition, spending $120M to become independent.
CCP Games is rebranding under the new entity name, Fenris Creations.

stories.title: Taming Outlier Tokens in Diffusion Transformers
stories.author: Chen Wei
bullet_points: The research investigates 'outlier tokens' within Diffusion Transformers (DiTs) used for image generation.
It addresses the phenomenon where Vision Transformers (ViTs) generate high-norm tokens that draw excessive attention but lack local detail.
The study confirms this outlier token behavior exists in both the encoder and denoiser components of RAE-DiT pipelines.

stories.title: LongSeeker: Elastic Context Orchestration for Long-Horizon Search Agents
stories.author: Siheng Chen
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of managing rapidly growing working context in long-horizon search agents.
Proposes adaptive context management, maintaining trajectory parts at different levels of detail based on relevance.
Introduces 'Context-ReAct' as a method to operationalize this adaptive context principle.

stories.title: When Life Gives You BC, Make Q-functions: Extracting Q-values from Behavior Cloning for On-Robot Reinforcement Learning
stories.author: Thomas Weng
bullet_points: The paper addresses a key limitation of Behavior Cloning (BC) in robotics: the lack of self-guided online improvement after initial data collection.
It introduces Q2RL, an algorithm designed to extract Q-values from BC demonstrations to facilitate efficient offline-to-online learning.
Q2RL aims to mitigate policy degradation caused by distribution mismatch when transitioning from static offline data to dynamic online learning.

stories.title: Geometry-Aware State Space Model: A New Paradigm for Whole-Slide Image Representation
stories.author: Fei Xia
bullet_points: The research addresses the challenge of analyzing gigapixel Whole-Slide Images (WSIs) in histopathology for disease diagnosis.
It introduces a 'Geometry-Aware State Space Model' as a new paradigm for representing WSI data.
The work aims to improve upon existing Multiple Instance Learning (MIL) methods by addressing limitations in patch embedding.

stories.title: Aes3D: Aesthetic Assessment in 3D Gaussian Splatting
stories.author: Weidong Cai
bullet_points: Addresses a gap in 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) evaluation by focusing on aesthetics rather than just reconstruction fidelity.
Proposes 'Aes3D' to assess higher-level visual attributes like composition and harmony in 3D scenes.
Highlights the need for new metrics as 3DGS becomes crucial for immersive media and digital content creation.

stories.title: Taming Outlier Tokens in Diffusion Transformers
stories.author: Chen Wei
bullet_points: The research investigates 'outlier tokens' within Diffusion Transformers (DiTs) used for image generation.
It addresses the phenomenon where high-norm tokens in ViTs carry disproportionate attention but limited local information.
The study confirms this outlier behavior exists in both the encoder and denoiser components of RAE-DiT pipelines.

stories.title: Estimating the expected output of wide random MLPs more efficiently than sampling
stories.author: Paul Christiano
bullet_points: Proposes a new method to estimate the expected output of wide Random MLPs without requiring network sampling.
The technique relies on approximating the distributions of activations across layers using tools like cumulants and Hermite polynomials.
This offers a potentially more efficient alternative to traditional Monte Carlo sampling for estimating expected loss in ML models.

stories.title: Understanding In-Context Learning for Nonlinear Regression with Transformers: Attention as Featurizer
stories.author: Rongjie Lai
bullet_points: The paper investigates In-Context Learning (ICL) in the context of nonlinear regression using Transformers.
It addresses a gap in existing ICL theory, which has largely focused on linear models.
The research explores how the attention mechanism within Transformers acts as a featurizer to enable ICL without weight updates.

stories.title: Superposition Is Not Necessary: A Mechanistic Interpretability Analysis of Transformer Representations for Time Series Forecasting
stories.author: Alper Yıldırım
bullet_points: The paper investigates how Transformer models, dominant in NLP, function when applied to time series forecasting.
It addresses the gap in understanding why simpler models (like DLinear) remain competitive against complex Transformers in this domain.
The research uses Sparse Autoencoders (SAEs) from mechanistic interpretability to probe the internal workings of the Transformer representations.

stories.title: The First Token Knows: Single-Decode Confidence for Hallucination Detection
stories.author: Mina Gabriel
bullet_points: Introduces 'first-token confidence' ($\phi_{first}$) as a novel method for detecting AI hallucinations.
Addresses limitations of existing methods like self-consistency, which require multiple costly decoding passes.
Proposes a computationally efficient alternative by analyzing the normalized entropy of top-K logits at the initial token.

stories.title: Beyond Semantics: An Evidential Reasoning-Aware Multi-View Learning Framework for Trustworthy Mental Health Prediction
stories.author: Mengling Feng
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of deploying LLM-based mental health prediction in real-world, high-stakes settings.
Proposes an Evidential Reasoning-Aware Multi-View Learning Framework to improve trustworthiness.
Focuses on overcoming limitations of current methods, such as overconfident predictions and lack of reliable uncertainty estimation.

stories.title: Continual Knowledge Updating in LLM Systems: Learning Through Multi-Timescale Memory Dynamics
stories.author: Constantine Dovrolis
bullet_points: Proposes a new paradigm for LLM external memory, moving beyond explicit management.
Draws inspiration from biological memory, suggesting multi-timescale dynamics for adaptive learning.
Introduces 'Memini,' an associative memory system designed to allow LLMs to continuously update knowledge naturally.

stories.title: Automatically Finding and Validating Unexpected Side-Effects of Interventions on Language Models
stories.author: Xiaoli Fern
bullet_points: Introduces an automated, contrastive evaluation pipeline for auditing LLM behavior.
The method compares generations from a base model and an intervention model across aligned prompts.
It generates human-readable, statistically validated hypotheses describing behavioral differences.

stories.title: The Pinocchio Dimension: Phenomenality of Experience as the Primary Axis of LLM Psychometric Differences
stories.author: Marcin Moskalewicz
bullet_points: Researchers used psychometric questionnaires on 50 LLMs to map out differences in their 'psychological' profiles.
The primary axis of variance found separates models based on their capacity to simulate phenomenally rich experience (e.g., embodied sensation, empathy).
This suggests a key dimension of LLM difference relates to the simulation of subjective, felt experience versus mere stimulus-driven reactivity.

stories.title: cursed_browser: A web browser with no rendering engine — the VLM reads the HTML and hallucinates the page
stories.author: github.com via aoeu
bullet_points: Introduces 'cursed_browser,' a novel web browser design.
The browser operates without a traditional rendering engine.
It utilizes a Vision-Language Model (VLM) to interpret HTML and 'hallucinate' the visual page.

stories.title: I Programmed an AI in 6502 Assembly - It Worked
stories.author: newellpaul
bullet_points: The article details the programming of an AI using the vintage 6502 assembly language.
It draws a historical comparison between the 6502's 1975 capabilities and modern AI tools like Claude Code.
The topic is highly niche, appealing to retro-computing enthusiasts and low-level programming experts.
